title: Tauri Desktop Replacement Parity
problem: The Tauri desktop client is the desired egui replacement. Windows packaged replacement gates are implemented and closed; macOS/Linux packaged parity, signed updater operations, and eventual egui removal remain follow-ons.
gates: pnpm test and pnpm build in apps/desktop for frontend changes
cargo fmt --check, cargo check -p codex-helper-desktop, and cargo nextest run -p codex-helper-desktop --lib for Tauri changes
pnpm tauri:build and packaged lifecycle smoke before replacement claims
review-workstream and verify-rust-workstream before lane closeout

evidence.summary: Opened replacement parity lane from the closed Tauri desktop readiness report and user goal. First executable task is TDRP-020: path opener actions plus lightweight single-config export/import.

Completed TDRP-040 with concerns. Windows NSIS packaging now bundles codex-helper as a Tauri external binary sidecar, start_desktop_proxy resolves packaged resources before development fallbacks, pnpm tauri:build produced the installer, and 7z confirmed the bundled sidecar. Full live packaged lifecycle smoke remains TDRP-080.

Completed TDRP-050 with concerns. Launch-at-login now uses tauri-plugin-autostart and the Settings switch is backed by the real plugin guest binding. Frontend tests/build and desktop Rust check/nextest pass. Manual packaged login-item smoke remains TDRP-080.

Completed TDRP-060 with concerns. First replacement release policy is manual GitHub Releases installer download. Auto-update is intentionally deferred until updater signing keypair, HTTPS endpoint, artifact hosting, and rollback operations are real. Settings shows disabled honest update copy.

Completed TDRP-070 with concerns. Single-endpoint provider common edits now patch alias, base_url, enabled, and optional env var names through a validated Tauri command while preserving advanced TOML fields. Multi-endpoint providers remain explicitly raw TOML.

Completed TDRP-080 with concerns. Windows NSIS packaged smoke now proves installed sidecar startup, close-to-tray, native tray Show/Hide/Quit menu paths, config export/import, Provider common edit UI, launch-at-login registration, detach, owned Stop Proxy, second-launch restore, and Quit App leaving the sidecar running in isolated temp homes.

Completed TDRP-090 with concerns. README, README_EN, CHANGELOG, and docs/DESKTOP_RELEASE now present Tauri as the verified Windows packaged GUI replacement path, keep macOS/Linux packaged parity and signed auto-update as explicit follow-ons, and mark codex-helper-gui/egui as a deprecated legacy fallback.

Completed TDRP-100 with concerns. Final verification passed for the current diff. The lane is closed for the Windows packaged replacement scope; macOS/Linux packaged smoke, signed updater operations, and eventual egui removal are explicit follow-ons.

continue_policy.notes: Closed for Windows packaged replacement scope. Open follow-on workstreams for macOS/Linux packaged smoke, signed updater release operations, or eventual egui removal when ready.
